rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Notice was not given to paternal first cousins once removed, Michael Ray Shoe and William Keith Shoe. 2) The whereabouts are reportedly unknown of paternal first cousins twice removed, Samantha Taylor, Jessica Taylor, and Allisia Taylor, although Petitioner has an address for their sister, Milissa Delfinado, and notice was given to Milissa Delfinado, albeit without the instructions for remote appearance attached to the notice. 3) The whereabouts of paternal first cousins once removed, Christopher Jones of New York and Robert Jones of North Carolina, are also reportedly unknown. 4) This Court ordinarily requires an ex parte petition to dispense with notice to an interested party whose whereabouts are unknown. [CRC 7.52.] None has been filed. 5) Twelve additionally discovered heirs were served notice of this amended petition but without the instructions for how to appear remotely. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] 6) Maternal second cousin Betty Butterfield reportedly died in 2023, so she post-deceased the decedent. Her successor-in-interest is not necessarily her issue (Bob Butterfield and Donna Butterfield Bateman). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KWAN K.LEE AND LAN H. LEE 2009 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Probate Code section 15403(b) provides in relevant part that: "If the continuance of the trust is necessary to carry out a material purpose of the trust, the trust cannot be modified or terminated unless the court, in its discretion, determines that the reason for doing so under the circumstances outweighs the interest in accomplishing a material purpose of the trust." When the settlors created the subject trust in 2009, the combined value of the settlors' estate was $1,000,000. The drafting attorney's declaration states that "concerns of potential estate tax was a motivating factor for the AB trust format at the time", even though the estate tax exemption available in 2009 was already up to $3,500,000 per spouse and $7,000,000 for both spouses. Was the settlors' adoption of the AB trust structure intended to be a material nontax purpose of preserving the predeceased settlor/spouse's share of trust assets for the ultimate benefit of their children, by limiting the surviving settlor/spouse's access to the irrevocable sub-trust? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
